fields.description:
Wire the two-scope gate posture across all three gates, plumb resolution-class + diagnostic through the verdicts, define the exit-code-11 contract, own the shared-file integration, enforce never-certify structurally, and document the model.

## Why
The three gates must share the systemic block-but-retryable posture while PRESERVING per-criterion fail-open, carry the `resolution_class` + `diagnostic` through the verdict/coverage so routing and troubleshooting both work, expose a distinct-but-grounded exit code, never sign a degraded result, and tell the human exactly what to do. This story is also the INTEGRATION OWNER for the shared seams the other children touch.

## What
- **Two-scope posture:** systemic failure -> block-but-retryable (exit 11, unsigned) for all three gates: make code-review match plan-review's existing systemic block; turn completion's fail-closed-as-FAIL into a distinct retryable exit (NOT a verification FAIL, which reads as "your work is wrong"). PRESERVE plan-review's per-criterion drop-and-continue fail-open (one flaky finder among N must not block the claim).
- **Verdict-schema field (concrete + rollback-safe):** add an OPTIONAL `coverage.resolution_class` (enum of the 8 closed values) + `coverage.diagnostic` (sanitized object) to all three verdict schemas (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json`, `code_review_verdict.schema.json`, `completion_verdict.schema.json`). Absent field = a normal (non-degraded) verdict. Forward/rollback-safe: the field is additive + optional and readers already tolerate unknown coverage keys, so a verdict written in a forward window and read after a rollback is simply ignored — no scrub/migration. **This story is the sole writer of these schema edits.** Naming: the concept is "resolution-disposition"; the persisted/field name is `resolution_class` — one field, not two.
- **Exit-code-11 contract — ALL THREE gate CLIs (not just review-plan):** the exit mapping is applied at each gate's CLI exit site, reading `coverage.resolution_class`: `WAIT_AND_RETRY`/`RETRY_NOW` -> exit 11 ("transient — retry"); every other non-null class -> the existing INDETERMINATE exit (2) with that class's message; null -> existing PASS/BLOCK exit. Sites to update, each with a CLI test: `rebar review-plan` (`_llm_commands.py:376`, currently `0 PASS / 1 BLOCK / 2 INDETERMINATE`); `rebar verify-completion` (currently `0 PASS else 1` — add the INDETERMINATE/retryable branch); and the completion close gate in `transition_close.py`. Data flow: the typed failure (story 30ff) sets `coverage.resolution_class` in the degraded-verdict dict; the CLI reads that key — so the field must exist before any CLI maps it (schema owned here). Worked example: a 429-driven degraded plan-review carries `coverage.resolution_class = "WAIT_AND_RETRY"` -> `rebar review-plan` exits 11; the same coverage on a completion close -> the close command exits 11, not 1. **`docs/exit-codes.md` is FROZEN (2026-06-09 window) and pinned by `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py`; codes 0/1/2/10 are load-bearing (exit 2 = usage error, NOT INDETERMINATE).** Adding exit 11 therefore MUST: (a) amend `docs/exit-codes.md` via its "Recorded decisions" process with the 11 rationale; (b) update `test_exit_codes.py` to pin 11; (c) enumerate exit-code consumers (CI scripts, the Gerrit reviewer-bot pipeline) and confirm additive-safety (any-non-zero-is-failure consumers are unaffected; only code-specific special-casers need updating); (d) document rollback = revert the exit-11 mapping to the prior INDETERMINATE exit. Record an ADR for the exit-11 contract (one-way CLI-surface door).
- **Never-sign structural guard (concrete + consumer-safe):** in the attestation/`sign_manifest` path, assert `verdict.verdict == "PASS" and verdict.coverage.get("resolution_class") is None` before signing; otherwise raise (a degraded/INDETERMINATE verdict is structurally unsignable). Consumer impact: this does NOT introduce a new unsigned state — today INDETERMINATE verdicts are ALREADY unsigned (only certifiable PASS is signed), so no consumer that reads attestations newly breaks; the guard just makes the existing "degraded => no attestation" invariant structural rather than incidental. Re-check the material fingerprint immediately before signing (retries + timeout widen the verify->sign window; avoid attesting against drifted state). Keep verify strictly OUTSIDE the write lock (`transition_close.py` current ordering preserved).
- **Integration ownership (shared files):** `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` is modified by five children (30ff, 95a5, fd1d, 978d, 9a82) at distinct but adjacent seams (except-seam classification; transport wiring at model construction; timeout/step-cap wiring; output validation; construction-time capability check), and `config.py`/`_config_schema.py`/the verdict schemas/docs by several. This story lands LAST. Explicit wave assignment by alias: **wave 0** = `gnomish-nosophobic-arawana` (RED baseline, pins current behavior — must land first so later stories flip its cases green); **wave 1** = `civilized-immediate-mamba` (classifier/`LLMOutcome`); **wave 2 (parallel)** = `morbid-uncultured-arcticduck` (retry transport + shared client — its spike gates the wave), `chief-contained-hoopoe` (timeouts into that client), `polite-dutiful-drake` (silent-success, consumes mamba's `LLMOutcome`), `sorry-clay-anole` (build-time lints); **wave 3** = this story (`authorial-hated-blackbear`). Intra-wave edges: arcticduck's spike before hoopoe's shared-client timeout; mamba's enum before drake's routing. This story owns the final `runner.py` reconciliation, and is the single-source-of-truth for the config namespace, the verdict-schema edits, and the taxonomy/retry/timeout docs. Per-child config-key docs defer to the consolidated doc here. Each contributing child names its exact `runner.py` seam in its description to keep the merges non-overlapping.
- **Session-log sink ownership:** writing the sanitized `diagnostic` to the session log (`log_session`/`append_session_log`) is owned by THIS story (previously named as a sink with no owner).
- **MCP surface (concrete):** the write-gated MCP gate tools return the failure as a structured dict carrying `resolution_class` + the class-specific message (mirroring the CLI exit-11 semantics), not an opaque error string — so MCP callers branch on the same disposition. (This replaces the earlier vague "MCP error shape" reference.)
- Per-class actionable messages + exit code 11. ADRs recorded HERE (sole owner; content authored by the noted stories): exit-11 contract; diagnostic-sanitization contract (mechanism in 30ff); deferred async watchdog (content in fd1d); transport-retry side-effect-safety architecture (content in 95a5). (No scope-vs-title ADR — the title was broadened instead.) Terminology: "signed" and "certified" denote the SAME act (writing an HMAC attestation); the plan uses "signed/attested" as canonical and treats "certify" as a synonym.

## Scope
- `gate_dispatch.py` wiring; `transition_close.py` (preserve verify-outside-lock ordering + add pre-sign fingerprint re-check); the verdict JSON schemas; `docs/` + `docs/adr/`.
- NOT in scope: removing or weakening per-criterion fail-open (must be preserved).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All three gates: a systemic failure -> block-but-retryable, exit 11 for retryable classes (else the existing INDETERMINATE exit), unsigned, with a class-specific actionable message; exit-code mapping is driven by `coverage.resolution_class` and covered by tests.
- [ ] The frozen exit-code contract is amended properly: `docs/exit-codes.md` recorded-decision entry added, `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py` updated to pin 11, and exit-code consumers (CI, Gerrit reviewer-bot) enumerated + confirmed additive-safe with a stated rollback.
- [ ] Per-criterion fail-open is preserved (a single flaky unit among N does not block; regression test).
- [ ] `resolution_class` + sanitized `diagnostic` are plumbed through the verdict schemas + `coverage` + the session log; this story is the only writer of the schema change.
- [ ] The structural never-sign guard rejects signing any degraded/INDETERMINATE verdict; the material fingerprint is re-checked before signing.
- [ ] Verify remains outside the write lock (ordering preserved; asserted).
- [ ] ADRs recorded (exit-11 contract, diagnostic sanitization, deferred async watchdog) — no scope-vs-title ADR (title broadened instead); the session-log diagnostic write and the structured MCP return are implemented; consolidated docs updated; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py`, `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py`, `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (exit-code mapping), `src/rebar/schemas/*.schema.json`, `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (final shared-seam reconciliation), the attestation/`sign_manifest` path, `docs/exit-codes.md`, `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py`, `docs/`, `docs/adr/`.
